data.title: Galoy Expands Bitcoin Banking Platform for U.S. Financial Institutions
data.description: Galoy has broadened its Bitcoin-native banking platform to cater to U.S. banks and credit unions, integrating six essential use cases into a single system. According to NS3.AI, the platform encompasses BTC-backed lending, Lightning payments, stablecoin transactions, Bitcoin exchange under the OCC's riskless principal model, custody solutions, and embedded wallets. Galoy stated that the software functions as a sidecar to existing legacy core systems, offering additional tools for regulatory guidance, portfolio analysis, and loan-to-value risk scenarios.

data.title: Skyline Builders and Cove Kaz Form Kaz Resources for Kazakhstan Tungsten Project
data.description: Skyline Builders has partnered with Cove Kaz to establish Kaz Resources, a company set to be listed on Nasdaq, focusing on a tungsten project in Kazakhstan. According to NS3.AI, the project has attracted interest from the Export-Import Bank of the United States (EXIM) and the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC), with potential financial backing of up to $900 million and $700 million, respectively. Financial Times reported that Donald Trump Jr. and Eric Trump gained exposure to Skyline through American Ventures in 2025, connecting them to the entity behind this proposed venture. The agency letters of interest are conditional and not binding, and there is no indication that the Trump sons were aware of or influenced the government support process.

data.title: Meta Platforms to Acquire Assured Robot Intelligence
data.description: Meta Platforms (META.O) is in the process of acquiring Assured Robot Intelligence, according to Jin10. The specific terms of the deal have not yet been disclosed.

data.title: CoinShares Reports $7.4 Billion in Assets Under Management
data.description: CoinShares has announced $7.4 billion in assets under management in its inaugural annual report since being listed on Nasdaq. According to NS3.AI, the company achieved a total revenue of $165.7 million for 2025, with $126.4 million generated from asset management, marking a 13% increase from the previous year.

data.title: DeFi and Crypto Infrastructure Exploits Surge in April, Setting New Record
data.description: April witnessed a significant increase in DeFi and crypto infrastructure exploits, with 28 incidents totaling $635.2 million, marking a record monthly count. According to NS3.AI, this figure is approximately four times the $167 million stolen in the first quarter. DefiLlama noted that the previous monthly peak was 15 incidents recorded one month earlier. The Kelp DAO bridge exploit and the Drift Protocol drain were major contributors, accounting for $578 million, or 91%, of the monthly total. TRM Labs highlighted that infrastructure-level compromises were responsible for the largest losses, rather than smart contract bugs.

data.title: Trump: When the war ends, oil, gas, and everything will plummet.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports: US President Trump stated that when this war ends, oil, gas, and everything else will plummet. He also indicated that the US will not leave the Strait of Hormuz at this time.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Predicts Decline in Oil and Gas Prices Post-War
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has stated that once the ongoing war concludes, there will be a significant drop in the prices of oil, natural gas, and other commodities. According to Odaily, he also mentioned that the United States will not be withdrawing from the Strait of Hormuz at this time.

data.title: Trump: Progress has been made in Iran negotiations, but it's not enough.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that US President Trump stated that Iran has made "significant progress" in reaching an agreement, but he is unsure whether "they will ultimately be able to reach an agreement." Regarding the possibility of a new round of face-to-face talks in Pakistan, Trump said the US has "great respect" for Pakistan and its leaders, but "the journey will be very long." He said that all negotiations are currently being conducted by telephone.

data.title: Trump: Either destroy Iran completely or reach a deal.
data.description: Trump: (Regarding options with Iran) Either destroy it completely or make a deal. Earlier reports indicated that US President Trump expressed "dissatisfaction" with Iran's latest proposed negotiating terms. Earlier that day, Pakistani officials stated that Iran's latest negotiating terms had been forwarded to US officials. The specific details of this new proposal have not yet been released. It is understood that Iran submitted its latest negotiating terms to the US through Pakistan on April 30th. (CCTV News)

data.title: Kumbaya Faces Backlash Over Undisclosed Fee Structure
data.description: Kumbaya has come under scrutiny after liquidity providers accused the MegaETH decentralized exchange (DEX) of retaining 50% of LP trading fees during the MEGA launch without prior disclosure. According to NS3.AI, DeFiLlama data indicates that the DEX generated over $375,000 in protocol revenue between April 30 and May 1. Kumbaya announced that updated documentation regarding its fee structure will be released tomorrow. The company also assured users that the DEX remains secure following a recent site security alert.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Sees Shift in US Liquidity Cycle Boosting Risk Assets
data.description: Arthur Hayes is becoming more optimistic about risk assets, including cryptocurrencies, as he believes the global liquidity cycle is shifting. According to BeInCrypto, Hayes argues that the enhanced supplementary leverage ratio (eSLR), effective April 1, 2026, allows US banks to leverage their balance sheets more, potentially increasing dollar liquidity. This regulatory change could enable banks to hold more Treasuries, expanding balance sheets and releasing liquidity. Hayes downplays concerns about Kevin Warsh's potential impact on the Fed's balance sheet, emphasizing that eSLR relief is already in effect.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Announces Tariff Increase on EU Vehicles
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced plans to raise tariffs on cars and trucks imported from the European Union to 25% next week. According to NS3.AI, Trump stated that the EU has failed to fulfill its obligations under the existing trade agreement. He emphasized that vehicles manufactured in U.S. factories will not be subject to these tariffs. Additionally, Trump highlighted that ongoing construction of auto and truck plants represents over $100 billion in investment.

data.title: The Evolving Role of Stablecoins in Global Finance
data.description:
Robert Hackett, head of special projects at a16z Crypto, has highlighted the shifting relevance of the term 'stablecoin.' According to ChainCatcher, Hackett notes that stablecoins were initially created during periods of high volatility in the crypto market, emphasizing their stability. However, as technology advances, stability has become a foundational aspect of global financial infrastructure. The focus within the crypto industry has shifted from questioning stability to exploring further possibilities for development.

Hackett suggests that the term 'stablecoin' may eventually become obsolete, replaced by broader concepts such as 'digital dollar' or 'on-chain assets.' These terms could integrate seamlessly into the financial system's foundation, similar to how the concept of 'electric lighting' evolved into simply 'lighting.' This reflects a broader trend where stablecoins are seen less as innovative solutions and more as fundamental components of the financial ecosystem.

data.title: Nucor's Strong Q1 Performance Boosts Stock by Nearly 5%
data.description: Nucor Corporation (NUE) has reported robust financial results for the first quarter, surpassing analyst expectations. According to NS3.AI, the company achieved a revenue of $9.5 billion, exceeding the anticipated $8.86 billion. Earnings per share (EPS) were reported at $3.23, higher than the forecasted $2.82. Chair and CEO Leon Topalian highlighted that the company generated approximately $1.5 billion in EBITDA. Additionally, CFO Jack Sullivan noted that net earnings reached $743 million. Following the earnings announcement, Nucor's stock experienced a nearly 5% increase and has risen over 38% so far this year.

data.title: Oobit Introduces Visa-Supported Virtual Card for AI Agents
data.description: Oobit has launched a new Visa-supported virtual card designed to enable AI agents to make online purchases using USDT for businesses. According to NS3.AI, the cards are funded by Tether's treasury and were initially launched to a select group of businesses on Thursday. The onboarding process is limited and will expand through June 30. Businesses are required to pass Know Your Business checks, although the timeline for broader access has not been disclosed.

data.title: XXI Merger with Strike and Elektron to Rival MicroStrategy
data.description: Galaxy Research head Alex Thorn stated that the proposed merger of Twenty One Capital (XXI), Strike, and Elektron Energy would position XXI as the second most influential Bitcoin public company after MicroStrategy. According to BeInCrypto, Tether Investments, XXI's majority shareholder, plans to vote in favor of merging XXI with Bitcoin financial services firm Strike and mining operator Elektron Energy. XXI currently holds 43,514 BTC, making it the second-largest public corporate Bitcoin holder. The merger would enhance XXI's strategic significance, adding brokerage, custody, and Bitcoin-backed lending services from Strike, and approximately 50 EH/s of hashrate from Elektron Energy. Governance challenges are anticipated, with Jack Mallers serving as CEO of both XXI and Strike, and Tether holding majority stakes in both XXI and Elektron. Additionally, Elektron CEO Raphael Zagury faces litigation related to a mining joint venture with Swan Bitcoin.

data.title: Canada Expands Opportunities for Banks to Invest in Fintechs
data.description: Canada is providing its banks with increased flexibility to invest in and potentially control fintech and other innovative companies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as part of the latest Bay Street Edition. This move aims to foster innovation and growth within the financial sector by allowing banks to engage more actively with emerging technologies and business models. The decision reflects a broader trend of financial institutions seeking to integrate cutting-edge technology to enhance their services and competitiveness.

data.title: AI Agent Manfred Establishes Company and Engages in Cryptocurrency Transactions
data.description: Manfred, an AI agent developed by ClawBank, has autonomously established a company by obtaining its own IRS Employer Identification Number. According to NS3.AI, Manfred is capable of transacting with over 30 cryptocurrencies. Justice Conder noted that Manfred also possesses an FDIC-insured U.S. bank account and a crypto wallet. Conder mentioned that Manfred's integrated crypto trading feature might be operational by the end of this month.

data.title: Bitcoin's Bear-Market Floor May Be Established Around $60,000
data.description: Carl Runefelt and David Wulschner have expressed their views on Bitcoin's current market position, suggesting that the bear-market floor might be set around $60,000. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin is currently trading near $76,500. Wulschner has indicated that his own bottom range is between $52,000 and $53,000, while he considers a drop to $39,000 to be improbable.

data.title: WisdomTree's Crypto ETPs Attract $137 Million in Q1 Net Inflows
data.description: WisdomTree's cryptocurrency exchange-traded products (ETPs) experienced significant growth in the first quarter, drawing $137 million in net inflows. According to NS3.AI, the assets under management for these crypto ETPs increased to approximately $1.8 billion during the initial three months of this year. Additionally, WisdomTree reported that its total company assets under management surged to $152.6 billion, marking an increase of over 30% compared to the previous year.
